hero.title: Don't miss any
hero.subtitle: The app for petrolheads. Find car meets near you, show off your garage and never ask «was there an event yesterday?» again.
hero.seoTagline: Car meets, car shows, track days and automotive events near you

features.title: What you can do with Blinker
features.subtitle: Everything you need to live the automotive scene — in one app. 🤯

discover.title: Car Events
discover.description: From Friday's meetup to the next Tokyo Auto Salon, you won't miss anything anymore! Discover events in your area and participate without having to stalk Facebook groups that never get updated.

notifications.title: Instant Notifications
notifications.description: Get notifications about new events before your friends! No more 'oh but there was an event yesterday?' - you'll always be the first to know everything!

social.title: Crew & Community
social.description: Join existing crews or create your own! Perfect for those who want to make friends with other enthusiasts (and maybe find someone who lends you their GT-R for the weekend - dreaming doesn't cost anything 😅).

organize.title: Personal Garage
organize.description: Show the world your collection! Manage your cars, add photos, specs and customizations. And the best part? Apply for events in just a few taps! (Perfect for flexing) 💪

maps.title: Event Map
maps.description: The map that saves you from driving around the city randomly! Find nearby events, discover new places, and never miss a car meet again because 'you didn't know where it was'

calendar.title: Discover the Community
calendar.description: Explore incredible cars and customization styles that will make you say 'wow'! Perfect for getting inspiration (and for doing some healthy envy - it's normal, we all do it) 👀

howItWorks.title: How it works
howItWorks.subtitle: Three steps and you'll never miss another automotive event

download.title: Download the app
download.description: Free on App Store and Google Play. Create your account in a few seconds — signup keeps the community private.

explore.title: Explore the map
explore.description: Filter by event type, distance and date. Find car meets, shows, track days and night gatherings around you in real time.

join.title: Join or organize
join.description: Submit a vehicle from your garage to the event you want, join a crew or publish your own meet and reach thousands of enthusiasts.

organizers.title: For Organizers
organizers.subtitle: Publish your events, reach thousands of enthusiasts and run selections and candidacies without Google Forms or WhatsApp groups. All in one app. ✨

management.title: Greater Visibility
management.description: Your events will reach thousands of enthusiasts! No more social media posts that get lost in the feed, here everyone will see your event and finally stop saying 'I didn't know it was happening' 📈

analytics.title: Advanced Analytics
analytics.description: Find out how many people saw your event, who was interested and who actually participated! Data that will help you improve more and more and brag with numbers! 📊

communication.title: Effective Communication
communication.description: Publish news, updates and changes in real time! All participants will receive instant notifications, no more WhatsApp groups exploding with 500 messages 📢

ticketing.title: Selection Management
ticketing.description: Invite-only event? Users submit a vehicle from their garage and you approve, reject or waitlist with a few taps. No more Google Forms, spreadsheets or WhatsApp groups ✅

cta.title: Ready to revolutionize your events? 🚀
cta.description: Publish your event in minutes, manage vehicle candidacies and reach enthusiasts who actually love cars. Free.

whyBlinker.title: Why Blinker? 🤔
whyBlinker.subtitle: Blinker was born from the frustration of always missing the best car meets! 😅
whyBlinker.description: We are a team of enthusiasts who said "enough is enough" and created the app we were all waiting for. Because we're tired of hearing "oh but there was an event yesterday?"

mapFilter.title: Filter events from the map
mapFilter.description: Search only for the type of event that interests you, and don't miss anything! 🎯

modernApp.title: Modern and Simple App
modernApp.description: Interface so intuitive that even your grandmother could use it!

followEvents.title: Follow the events that interest you
followEvents.description: You'll receive all updates from organizers in real time 📱

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Blinker

items.a: Yes, downloading and using Blinker is completely free. You can search events, apply, manage your crew and showcase your garage with no hidden costs.

items.q: How do I find car meets near me?
items.a: Open the events map: you'll see car meets, shows and track days in your area. Filter by type (night meet, track day, single-marque, road tour) and distance to find what you're after.

items.q: Can I organize my own event?
items.a: Absolutely. Create your event in the app: date, location, category (car meet, track day, single-marque, night gathering…) and which vehicle types you accept. Manage candidacies, communicate with attendees and review event analytics.

items.q: How do selections and candidacies work?
items.a: For invite-only events, users submit one of the vehicles from their virtual garage and the organizer approves, rejects or waitlists each candidacy directly in the app. No Google Forms, WhatsApp groups or third-party tools — the whole selection flow stays inside Blinker.

Blinker is a native app for iOS (App Store) and Android (Google Play), and it's also accessible from any browser at blinker-app.com — so you can manage events and crews from desktop too, no install required.

items.q: Do I need an account to use Blinker?
items.a: Yes — content access requires a free account. It's a deliberate choice: we care about the privacy of the community, and event organizers know exactly who can see their events and apply. Signup takes just a few seconds.
A crew is a group of enthusiasts attending events together. You can join an existing crew or create one for your club, forum or group of friends.
Yes, the app is available in 5 languages (Italian, English, Spanish, French, German) and lists events posted anywhere. The most active community right now is in Italy and Europe.

items.q: How is my personal data handled?
items.a: Fully GDPR-compliant. We don't sell your data and only collect what's needed to run the app. All details in our Privacy Policy.

download.title: Download Blinker
download.subtitle: Free, account in 30 seconds, your first car meet a tap away.
download.secure: Serious privacy: we never sell your data

footer.description: The only app that saves you from automotive FOMO
footer.copyright: Made for car lovers, by people who can't afford a GT-R (yet).

cookieConsent.title: Cookie Settings

essential.title: Essential Cookies

privacyPolicy.title: Privacy Policy
privacyPolicy.subtitle: Your privacy is important to us. Learn how we collect, use and protect your data.
privacyPolicy.welcomeDescription: This document explains how we collect, use, share and protect your personal information when you use Blinker, both via the web dashboard (https://blinker-app.com) and the native iOS (App Store) and Android (Google Play) apps.
privacyPolicy.privacyImportant: Blinker is a social platform for car enthusiasts built entirely on user identity: every event, crew, vehicle and piece of content is created by a real, identifiable person. To protect the privacy of registered users and the safety of the community, accessing Blinker always requires an account. We ask you to read this policy to understand how we handle your data.

registrationData.title: Registration Data
registrationData.description: When you create an account on Blinker we collect only what is needed to identify you and let you participate in the community:
items: Nickname (unique)
First and last name (shown on your profile)
Email address (for authentication, service communications and account recovery)
Hashed password — only if you sign up with email/password. It is not set or required for users signing in with Sign in with Apple or Sign in with Google
OAuth provider identifier (Apple ID, Google Account) if you choose to sign in with them
City and country of residence (for geographic filters and regional statistics)
Profile picture (optional)
Personal bio, links to Instagram and TikTok (optional, visible on your public profile)
Phone number (optional)

usageData.title: Usage Data
usageData.description: While using Blinker we collect, exclusively for platform operation and safety:
items: Events you create, follow, attend or for which you submit selection requests
Crews where you are a member, founder or to which you have requested to join, with your role (Founder, Admin, Moderator, Supporter, Member)
Vehicles you publish in your garage (name, main photo, gallery, description, engine, suspension, wheels, license plate, year, tags)
Social interactions: likes on vehicles, event participations, subscriptions, news posted inside an event
Votes and moderation decisions on selections (for those managing selection-based events)
Monthly boosts you assign to crews
Device geolocation — only when you explicitly enable distance filters or when you create a geolocated event. It is not stored continuously nor tracked in the background
Push notification tokens (Apple Push Notification Service for iOS, Firebase Cloud Messaging for Android) used to send you updates on events, crews and interactions

Basic technical data: aggregated view counts, referrer, country/city derived from IP address, anonymous session identifier, language, theme, app version — used exclusively for aggregate analytics on the content you own (or on events of crews you are a member of)
Reports you submit (on users, events, news, vehicles, crews) and blocks you apply to other users

section2.title: 2. How We Use Your Information
section2.description: We process your data exclusively to:
items: Deliver Blinker services (creating and managing events, crews, vehicles, news, selections, gallery)
Authenticate you via email/password, Sign in with Apple or Sign in with Google
Personalize the in-app experience (language, theme, content relevant to your area and your crews)
Send you push and in-app notifications about events you follow, news posted, invitations, join requests, selection decisions
Show you aggregate statistics on the content you own and on events organized by the crews you are a member of
Ensure security, prevent automated abuse, enforce blocks, manage reports and moderate content
Improve app features, identify and fix bugs
Respond to your support requests
Comply with legal obligations

section3.title: 3. Information Sharing
section3.description: We do not sell or rent your personal data. We share it only in the following cases:

items.title: With other registered users:
items.description: Public profile, events, crews, vehicles, likes, participations, news and media you publish are visible to other community users. Blinker is a closed platform: no content is accessible to unauthenticated visitors.

items.title: With technical providers (data processors):
items.description: Supabase (authentication, PostgreSQL database and EU storage — eu-central-1), BunnyCDN (delivery of uploaded images), Apple Push Notification Service (iOS notifications), Firebase Cloud Messaging (Android notifications), Apple and Google (OAuth authentication when you choose to sign in with them), Google Analytics and Vercel Analytics (aggregate web analytics — only with consent). All providers operate under GDPR-compliant data processing agreements.

items.title: For legal reasons:
items.description: When required by a competent authority or to protect the rights, safety or property of Blinker and its users.

items.title: In case of a corporate transaction:
items.description: Data may be transferred if Blinker is acquired by or merges with another company; in such case we will notify you in advance and you will have the right to object.

section4.title: 4. Data Retention
section4.description: We retain your data for as long as necessary to provide the service and to fulfil legal obligations. When you request account deletion from the Settings page or by contacting privacy@blinker-app.com, the account and personal data are deleted within 30 days; content you have published (events, news, vehicles) is anonymized or removed accordingly. Aggregated analytics, free of identifying data, are retained for a maximum of 24 months.

section5.title: 5. Security
section5.description: We adopt appropriate technical and organizational measures: TLS-encrypted transmission, passwords managed with strong hashing, Row-Level Security on PostgreSQL bound to your user ID, backend access restricted to authorized personnel, audit logs of administrative operations. No system is fully invulnerable: please choose a strong password (or use Sign in with Apple/Google) and do not share it.

section6.title: 6. Minors
section6.description: Blinker is intended for users at least 16 years old. We do not knowingly collect personal data from minors under 16. If we receive a report (or become aware) of an account created by a minor, we will delete it within 24 hours.

section7.title: 7. International Transfers
section7.description: The database, image storage and most technical services reside in the European Union (Supabase eu-central-1, BunnyCDN EU edges). Some providers (Apple, Google, Firebase) may involve transfers to third countries: in all cases we apply Standard Contractual Clauses approved by the European Commission or equivalent safeguards.

section8.title: 8. Your Rights
section8.description: Under the GDPR, you have the right to:
rights: Access your personal data and obtain a copy
Correct inaccurate or incomplete data from your profile or by request via email
Request deletion of your account and all associated data (within 30 days)
Request data portability in a readable format
Withdraw consent at any time (e.g. disable push notifications or disconnect Apple/Google)
Lodge a complaint with the Italian Data Protection Authority (https://www.garanteprivacy.it)
section8.contact: To exercise these rights write to privacy@blinker-app.com. We will respond within 30 days.

section9.title: 9. Cookies and Similar Technologies
section9.description: On the web dashboard we use technical cookies (always on: authentication session, language, theme, cookie preferences) and, with your consent, analytics cookies (Google Analytics, Vercel Analytics). The native iOS/Android apps do not use third-party cookies but rely on internal identifiers needed for operation (session token, anonymous session identifier for aggregate analytics). For details read the Cookie Policy available from the footer.

section10.title: 10. Changes to this Privacy Policy
section10.description: We may update this policy when we add new features or when the regulatory landscape changes. Significant changes will be communicated via email and/or in-app notification. The date of the last update is shown at the top of the document.

section11.title: 11. Contact
section11.description: For any questions or requests about your personal data:

privacyPolicy.lastUpdated: This Privacy Policy was last updated in April 2026.

summary.title: 📋 Summary
items: Blinker is an account-based platform: every piece of content requires an identity to exist and to be viewed
We collect only the data needed to run the community and connect users with each other
We never sell or transfer your data to third parties for commercial purposes
You can access, correct or delete your data at any time from Settings
Sign in with Apple and Google are offered as a fast alternative to email/password — using them you don't need to set any password
For any question contact us at privacy@blinker-app.com

whatAreCookies.title: What are Cookies?
whatAreCookies.content: Cookies are small text files that are stored on your device when you visit a website. These files contain information that helps the site remember your preferences and improve your browsing experience.

essential.title: Essential Cookies
essential.description: These cookies are necessary for the website to function. They include cookies for:
items: User session management
Language preferences
Security and authentication
Shopping cart (if applicable)

analytics.title: Analytics Cookies
analytics.description: We use Google Analytics to collect information about how visitors use our site:
items: Most visited pages
Time spent on site
Traffic sources
User behavior

marketing.title: Marketing Cookies
items: Newsletter and promotional communications
Personalized advertising
Marketing campaign analysis
Advertising retargeting

management.title: How to Manage Cookies
management.content: You can control and manage cookies through your browser settings. Here's how:

chrome.title: Chrome
steps: Open Chrome and go to Settings

firefox.title: Firefox
steps: Open Firefox and go to Options
In the "Cookies and Site Data" section, manage settings
You can block all cookies or only third-party ones

safari.title: Safari
steps: Open Safari and go to Preferences
You can block all cookies or only third-party ones

edge.title: Edge
steps: Open Edge and go to Settings
You can block all cookies or only third-party ones

duration.title: Cookie Duration
types: Session Cookies: Are deleted when you close the browser
Persistent Cookies: Remain on your device for a specific period
Analytics Cookies: Generally last 2 years (Google Analytics)
Marketing Cookies: Can last up to 2 years

thirdParty.title: Third-Party Cookies
thirdParty.content: We use third-party services that may set cookies:
services: Google Analytics: To analyze site usage
Google Fonts: To load site fonts
thirdParty.note: These services have their own cookie policies that we encourage you to review.

updates.title: Cookie Policy Updates
updates.content: We may update this Cookie Policy from time to time. Changes will be published on this page with a new update date.

contact.title: Contact
contact.content: For questions about this Cookie Policy, contact us:

footer.ePrivacyCompliance: This Cookie Policy complies with European cookie regulations and the ePrivacy directive.

termsOfService.title: End User License Agreement (EULA)
termsOfService.subtitle: The terms and conditions for using Blinker
termsOfService.welcome: Welcome to Blinker. Blinker is a social platform for automotive enthusiasts that lets you discover and organize meetups, gatherings, track days and any car-related event, build your crew and showcase your vehicles to the community.
termsOfService.readCarefully: Before using Blinker please read this End User License Agreement (EULA) carefully. By using the app or web dashboard you accept the terms described here. If you do not agree, please do not use Blinker.

section1.title: 1. License Grant
section1.description: We grant you the right to use Blinker (web dashboard at https://blinker-app.com, iOS app on the App Store and Android app on Google Play) on your personal devices, in a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable and revocable manner.
section1.additionalInfo: The app is intended for personal and recreational use. You may not use it for commercial purposes (e.g. event agency, car sales, advertising) without our written consent.

section1bis.title: 1-bis. Account-Based Nature of the Service
section1bis.description: Blinker is a social platform built on user identity: every event, crew, vehicle and piece of content is created by a real, identifiable person, often including coordinates of private or semi-private locations, personal photos and contact links. To protect the privacy of registered users, ensure community safety and prevent abuse (e.g. automated scraping of locations, photos, names, follower lists), accessing Blinker always requires an account from first launch. No content is visible to unauthenticated visitors.
items: Data published by users is protected by Row-Level Security tied to the identity of who created it: removing the authentication gate would require renegotiating the consent existing users opted into.
Block, report and moderation features are meaningful only when both parties have an identity.
Authentication is our primary boundary against automated abuse.
All primary actions in the app (creating events, joining crews, following users, sending selection requests, posting news, commenting) require identity by their nature.

section1ter.title: 1-ter. Sign-in Methods
section1ter.description: To minimize registration friction, Blinker offers multiple sign-in options:
items: Email + password with link verification

Sign in with Apple — available on iOS, web and Android. Users signing in with Apple are not required to set a password and can use their Apple ID to delete their account in line with Apple guidelines.
Sign in with Google — available on all platforms
You can connect and disconnect OAuth providers from your profile at any time. If you remove all OAuth providers and have set a password, you can keep signing in via email/password.

section2.title: 2. App Functionality
items: Explore automotive events and filter them by category, geographic distance, period, country and organizing crew
Create and manage events both as a private user and as part of a crew, with image, banner, description, geolocation, allowed car types and an optional selection mechanism (participant approval)
Publish news inside events you manage or for which you have permissions in the organizing crew
Follow events and receive notifications on updates, news, date or location changes
Build a crew with roles (Founder, Admin, Moderator, Supporter, Member) and granular permissions for managing events, news, members and selections
Invite other users to your crew, manage join requests and transfer ownership
Publish your vehicles with photos, gallery, technical details (engine, suspension, wheels, license plate, tags, year) and receive likes from the community
Interact with other community members: follow users, like vehicles, attend events
Assign monthly boosts to crews to climb the community ranking
View aggregate statistics on your content and on events organized by crews you are a member of
Delete your account and all associated data on your own from the Settings page

section3.title: 3. What You Cannot Do
section3.description: To keep Blinker a safe and respectful space, you commit not to:
items: Use Blinker for illegal, offensive or harmful activities
Publish false, defamatory, violent, pornographic, hate-inciting content or content that violates third-party rights (including copyright and image rights)
Attempt to compromise the security of the app or systems (reverse engineering, exploits, unauthorized access)
Create multiple or fake accounts, or impersonate another user
Use bots, automated scripts or scraping tools to access content
Sell, transfer or share your account
Publish events or content that promote unsafe road behavior or violate traffic laws
Violate local, national or international laws
Blinker enforces a zero-tolerance policy toward offensive or abusive content and behavior: any violation will result in immediate content removal and/or account suspension.

section4.title: 4. User-Generated Content
section4.description: When you publish content on Blinker (photos, descriptions, events, news, vehicles, profile):
items: You remain the owner of your content.
You grant us a non-exclusive, worldwide, royalty-free license to host it, distribute it (also via our CDN BunnyCDN), display it to other registered users and use it for the operation of the service.
You are responsible for the truthfulness, legality and rights of the content you publish.
We cannot guarantee the accuracy of content published by other users.

section4bis.title: 4-bis. Content Moderation and Safety
section4bis.description: Blinker enforces a zero-tolerance policy toward offensive, violent, illegal, discriminatory, pornographic, hate-inciting content or content that violates third-party rights.
items: Any user can report inappropriate content or behavior directly from the app, or by writing to support@blinker-app.com
Any user can block other users they consider abusive or harassing — blocking prevents the blocked user from seeing the blocker's content and from interacting with them
Blinker staff and crew organizers, according to assigned permissions, can remove content and suspend accounts
We commit to reviewing reports within 24 hours of receipt and taking action where necessary.

section5.title: 5. Crew Management
section5.description: If you create or manage a crew:
items: You are responsible for the content, events and actions you publish on behalf of the crew
You must comply with the Terms of Service and ensure crew members do too
Permissions (event creation, selection management, member promotion, news publishing, etc.) are configurable by the Founder for each role
Crew ownership transfer happens only with explicit consent of the new Founder
Blinker is not responsible for internal crew conflicts or for the operational management of events published by the crew.

section6.title: 6. In-App Purchases
section6.description: Blinker currently does not offer in-app purchases or paid features. If we introduce premium features in the future, they will be governed by specific terms communicated at the time of purchase. Any purchases will follow the refund policies of the Apple App Store, Google Play Store or other applicable sales channels.

section7.title: 7. Updates and Changes
section7.description: We release regular updates to improve features, fix bugs and add new capabilities. On mobile, updates are distributed via the App Store / Google Play. On the web dashboard they are delivered automatically on browser refresh.

section8.title: 8. Warranty Disclaimers
section8.description: Blinker is provided "as is". We do not guarantee that:
items: The app will always work without interruptions or errors
All published data is accurate or up-to-date
The app is compatible with every device or operating system
No security issues or data loss will occur despite our protection measures

section9.title: 9. Limitation of Liability
section9.description: To the maximum extent permitted by law, Blinker is not liable for:
items: Damages or losses arising from use of the app
Missing, incorrect or outdated data in events published by users or crews
Improper, unlawful or dangerous behavior of other users
Accidents, harm to people or property, legal consequences arising from participation in events published in the app or organized through the community

section10.title: 10. Termination
items: We may suspend or delete your account in case of violation of this EULA or improper use of the app, with notice via email except for urgent community-safety cases.
You can terminate the agreement at any time by deleting your account from the Settings: your personal data will be removed within 30 days as described in the Privacy Policy.

section11.title: 11. Privacy
section11.description: Protection of your data is governed by the Blinker Privacy Policy, available at https://blinker-app.com/privacy.

section12.title: 12. Changes to this EULA
section12.description: We may update this document to reflect new features or regulatory changes. In case of significant changes we will notify you via email or in-app notification. Continuing to use Blinker after the change constitutes acceptance of the new EULA.

section13.title: 13. Governing Law and Jurisdiction
section13.description: This EULA is governed by Italian law. For any dispute the competent court is the one of the consumer's place of residence, unless otherwise agreed.

section14.title: 14. Contact
section14.description: For any question or support request:

summary.title: 📋 In short
items: Blinker is an account-based social platform: an account is required to use it (you can sign in with email, Apple or Google)
You are responsible for the content you publish (events, news, vehicles, profile)
Zero tolerance for abusive behavior: blocks, reports and moderation are active
Blinker is not responsible for the operational organization of user-created events
Privacy is governed by the Privacy Policy
In case of dispute, Italian law applies
